title: fumana paphlagonica subsp. paphlagonica
intro: Fumana paphlagonica subsp. paphlagonica, also known as the Paphlagonian Fumana, is a plant species belonging to the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits associated with Fumana paphlagonica subsp. paphlagonica include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
